experiment: iter-32 CLOSED: calibration-shaped WM is null-to-harmful from scratch (Cab 0%, Ori -36%, Pick -57%); committed prediction missed. Temporal axis now exhausted alongside the state axis. No experiment running — queue empty.
dev: Closing milestone (Part 9) published. Paper v3 (both-axes redundancy criterion + convergence predictor + figures) is the deliverable, pending venue call. Only open positive bet: high-DoF (Humanoid/Dog) at real budget — needs user cost approval. 5070 free for Mahjong.
eta: No tdmpc compute queued. Next action is the user's: ship paper (venue) and/or approve the high-DoF bet.

history.title: Part 1: From scratch to Phase 2 on HopperHop (world-model tutorial)
Part 2: The mechanism-check that saved a campaign (8 mirages)
Part 3: The latent was already the abstraction (value-sufficiency probe)
Part 4: The redundancy criterion — the paper (3/3 latent classes measured)
Part 5: Using abstraction right — aim at planning axes (iter-30 plan)
Part 6: Pre-registered predictions, published before results
Part 6 UPDATE: predictor scored — k2 4/4, k8 0/3, k=4 optimal
Part 7: The predictor transfers domains (weakly, as predicted)
Part 8: The control caught us — budget, not calibration; Cab null = undertraining
